instructions.instruction: Always read the Dockerfile first using the Read tool before running or recommending any validation commands.
instructions.why_given: The Mandatory Workflow section specifies 'Read the Dockerfile first — Use the Read tool to examine the Dockerfile before running validation' as step 1 of pre-validation.

instructions.instruction: Run the four-stage validation in order: [1/4] hadolint syntax, [2/4] Checkov security scan, [3/4] custom best practices checks, [4/4] optimization analysis.
instructions.relevant_when: Validating any Dockerfile using the dockerfile-validate.sh script.
instructions.why_given: The skill defines a fixed 4-stage pipeline and labels each stage numerically; the ordering matters because syntax issues should be resolved before security scanning.

instructions.instruction: Summarise findings by severity: Critical (security vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets), High (missing USER, HEALTHCHECK, :latest tags), Medium (layer optimization, version pinning), Low (style, informational).
instructions.relevant_when: Post-validation step when presenting results to the user.
instructions.why_given: The Mandatory Workflow post-validation section specifies exactly this four-tier severity categorisation.

instructions.instruction: Cache cleanup (rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* or apk --no-cache) MUST occur in the same RUN layer as the package installation, not in a separate RUN command.
instructions.relevant_when: Dockerfile uses apt-get, apt, or apk to install packages.
instructions.why_given: The Non-Obvious Checks table states 'Cache Cleanup: Must happen in the same RUN layer as the install; a separate RUN rm -rf creates a new layer that does not reduce size'.

instructions.instruction: COPY dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod) before copying source code so that dependency installation is cached separately from source changes.
instructions.relevant_when: Dockerfile installs application dependencies from a manifest file.
instructions.why_given: The Layer Ordering entry in the Non-Obvious Checks table and the Build Cache Efficiency section both require COPY of dependency files before source code.

instructions.instruction: Never treat a successful docker build as validation. Always run hadolint as a required check even when the build exits 0.
instructions.relevant_when: User reports the image builds successfully and asks whether validation is needed.
instructions.why_given: The Anti-Patterns section states 'NEVER skip Hadolint because the image builds successfully' with explicit BAD/GOOD examples.

instructions.instruction: After proposing fixes, ask the user whether they want the fixes applied before modifying any Dockerfile.
instructions.relevant_when: Fixes are ready to propose to the user.
instructions.why_given: The Mandatory Workflow step 3 states 'Offer to apply fixes — Ask the user if they want fixes applied, then apply if approved'.
